name: Biome Generation
description: A region and content-distribution generator that assigns biome identities, boundaries, environment rules, resources, hazards, encounter pools, and visual themes across a map or world.
design_notes: Biome generation owns what kind of region a chunk, room, or area belongs to; it does not own exact room placement. Useful biome systems track biome_id, region weights, climate inputs, resource and encounter pools, adjacency rules, transition widths, content_version, deterministic seed streams, and validation so the world reads as varied without producing impossible resource or traversal combinations.
design_purpose: Create recognizable regions with distinct visuals, resources, enemies, and traversal constraints.
Control pacing by placing safer, harsher, richer, or more specialized zones in meaningful patterns.
Support replayable worlds while keeping resource access and navigation solvable.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are crossing distinct regions with different moods, risks, and opportunities.

parameters.name: biome_id
parameters.description: Canonical biome rule set applied to terrain, decoration, encounters, and resources.

parameters.name: biome_noise_scale
parameters.description: Noise sampling scale used to define biome regions or masks.

parameters.name: biome_seed_stream_key
parameters.description: RNG stream used for biome assignment so region layout can be replayed independently from encounters or loot.

parameters.name: region_weight_table_id
parameters.description: Weight table that controls biome frequency by progression, climate band, or world region.

parameters.name: biome_content_pool_id
parameters.description: Content pool used for resources, enemies, hazards, props, or weather inside the biome.

parameters.name: transition_width_tiles
parameters.description: Blend width between adjacent biome masks or material zones.

parameters.name: adjacency_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that controls which biome pairs can touch or blend.

parameters.name: resource_density_multiplier
parameters.description: Biome-specific modifier for resource, loot, or encounter placement density.

parameters.name: biome_content_version
parameters.description: Version of biome rules and content pools used to keep old saves and debug seeds reproducible.

edge_cases: A required resource appears only in a biome that the seed failed to place near reachable progression routes.
Biome transitions create narrow hazard strips, visual seams, or mismatched collision/navigation rules.
Adjacent biome rules reject every candidate and require fallback_template_id or repair passes.
Content patches change biome_id rules and old saves need compatibility for discovered map regions.
Hazardous biome placement can gate critical resources behind temperature, oxygen, traversal, or equipment requirements.
Chunk streaming can sample neighboring biome masks at different content versions and create seams or duplicated resources.
Transition zones need clear ownership for weather, enemy pools, music, minimap color, and survival exposure effects.
common_bugs: Biome masks use different coordinates than terrain chunks, causing decoration and ground textures to disagree.
Resource placement samples biome_id before transition blending and spawns desert resources in forest visuals.
RNG stream sharing means adding a new biome changes loot or encounter generation elsewhere.
Debug maps show colors but not adjacency_rule_id or rejected placement reasons.
Only biome_id is saved, so changed region weights regenerate resources or hazards differently after reload.
Transition tiles inherit visuals from one biome and collision, weather, or encounter rules from another.
balancing_notes: Biome size should support travel goals: tiny regions feel noisy, while huge regions can starve players of needed resources.
Resource density and enemy difficulty need to be tuned per biome so visual variety also affects play.
Rare biomes are exciting only if players have clues, maps, or systems that make finding them reasonable.
accessibility_notes: Use shapes, landmarks, labels, and material changes in addition to color to distinguish biome boundaries.
Avoid dense procedural decoration that hides hazards, enemies, or interactables without visibility options.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate biome masks from chunk_coordinate, biome_seed_stream_key, and biome_content_version, then pass biome_id into terrain, foliage, resource, weather, and encounter systems; draw editor overlays for masks and adjacency failures.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep biome rules in Resources or JSON and sample them before TileMap or mesh decoration; save biome_content_version, discovered biome IDs, and transition ownership for map compatibility.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use PCG graphs or custom runtime systems with biome_id metadata; validate biome adjacency, navigation, hazard gates, and spawn/resource rules before streaming a region active.
implementation_notes.web: Use deterministic noise and integer chunk coordinates to assign biome_id; export biome masks, region weights, and rejected adjacency reasons so bad seeds can be reproduced from seed and content version.

relationships.reason: Biome masks, region weights, and content pools need deterministic seed streams and content versions for reproducible worlds.
Biome generation produces region masks and biome IDs that terrain, materials, props, and hazards consume.
Biome state constrains encounter pools so enemies, hazards, and rewards fit the current region.
Biome climate and weather rules can drive exposure, heat, cold, and shelter pressure in survival loops.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses generated biomes with distinct terrain and resources.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses biome regions with specific enemies and materials.
